title: GLBA §314.4(b) — Risk assessment: information-security risk identification and tracking
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=GLBA, clause=§314.4(b), available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §314.4(b).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GLBA §314.4(b) (Risk assessment) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.37: GLBA §314.4(b) — Risk assessment: information-security risk identification and tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.37' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Risk-assessment programme exports, vulnerability scan results, threat-intelligence feeds, GRC platform risk registers.
spl:
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (risk_assessment, vuln_scan_summary) earliest=-90d
| eval risk_identified=if(isnotnull(risk_id) AND risk_rating IN ("critical", "high", "medium", "low"), 1, 0)
| eval risk_mitigated=if(mitigation_status IN ("mitigated", "accepted", "transferred"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_risks sum(risk_identified) AS identified sum(risk_mitigated) AS mitigated dc(asset_category) AS asset_categories_assessed latest(assessment_date) AS last_assessment BY business_function
| eval unmitigated=identified - mitigated
| eval mitigation_pct=round(100*mitigated/identified, 1)
| eval days_since_assessment=round((now()-strptime(last_assessment, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400)
| where unmitigated>0 OR days_since_assessment>365
| table business_function, total_risks, identified, mitigated, unmitigated, mitigation_pct, days_since_assessment, asset_categories_assessed
description: Monitors information-security risk-assessment coverage, finding resolution rates, and assessment currency across business functions per GLBA §314.4(b) requirements.
value: §314.4(b) requires financial institutions to conduct risk assessments identifying reasonably foreseeable internal and external risks. Continuous tracking ensures the risk-assessment programme stays current and findings are addressed.
implementation: (1) Ingest risk-assessment and vulnerability-scan results with risk_id, risk_rating, mitigation_status; (2) schedule quarterly; (3) escalate overdue assessments and unmitigated high risks. Indexes required: `index=grc_compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=risk_assessment`, `sourcetype=vuln_scan_summary`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `asset_categories_assessed`, `business_function`, `days_since_assessment`, `identified`, `last_assessment`, `mitigated`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Risk-assessment programme exports, vulnerability scan results, threat-intelligence feeds, GRC platform risk registers.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest risk-assessment and vulnerability-scan results with risk_id, risk_rating, mitigation_status; (2) schedule quarterly; (3) escalate overdue assessments and unmitigated high risks.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (risk_assessment, vuln_scan_summary) earliest=-90d
| eval risk_identified=if(isnotnull(risk_id) AND risk_rating IN ("critical", "high", "medium", "low"), 1, 0)
| eval risk_mitigated=if(mitigation_status IN ("mitigated", "accepted", "transferred"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_risks sum(risk_identified) AS identified sum(risk_mitigated) AS mitigated dc(asset_category) AS asset_categories_assessed latest(assessment_date) AS last_assessment BY business_function
| eval unmitigated=identified - mitigated
| eval mitigation_pct=round(100*mitigated/identified, 1)
| eval days_since_assessment=round((now()-strptime(last_assessment, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400)
| where unmitigated>0 OR days_since_assessment>365
| table business_function, total_risks, identified, mitigated, unmitigated, mitigation_pct, days_since_assessment, asset_categories_assessed
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
